1. The width of the golden rectangle is given as 2 meters. The golden ratio φ is defined as φ = (1 + √5) / 2 ≈ 1.618. The length of the rectangle can be calculated using the definition of the golden ratio:  
$$
\text{length} = \phi \times \text{width} = \phi \times 2 = 2\phi
$$  
The area of the rectangle is then:
$$
\text{Area} = \text{length} \times \text{width} = 2\phi \times 2 = 4\phi
$$  
Using the exact value of φ, the area is:
$$
\text{Area} = 4 \times \frac{1 + \sqrt{5}}{2} = 2(1 + \sqrt{5}) = 2 + 2\sqrt{5}
$$  
The diagonal of the rectangle can be found using the Pythagorean theorem:
$$
\text{Diagonal} = \sqrt{\text{length}^2 + \text{width}^2} = \sqrt{(2\phi)^2 + 2^2} = \sqrt{4\phi^2 + 4}
$$  
Substituting φ = (1 + √5)/2:
$$
\text{Diagonal} = \sqrt{4 \left( \frac{1 + \sqrt{5}}{2} \right)^2 + 4} = \sqrt{4 \left( \frac{1 + 2\sqrt{5} + 5}{4} \right) + 4} = \sqrt{(1 + 2\sqrt{5} + 5) + 4} = \sqrt{10 + 2\sqrt{5}}
$$

2. The circle inscribed within the golden rectangle will have a diameter equal to the width of the rectangle, which is 2 meters. Therefore, the radius of the circle is:
$$
r = \frac{2}{2} = 1 \text{ meter}
$$  
The area of the circle is:
$$
\text{Area of circle} = \pi r^2 = \pi \times 1^2 = \pi
$$  
The area of the region inside the rectangle but outside the circle is the area of the rectangle minus the area of the circle:
$$
